reasoning: The anchor price will be $69.41 (from Friday, March 28, 2025, since March 30 is a Sunday). This price reflects a massive ~7.5% single-day jump following the announcement that Japanese insurer Mitsui Sumitomo intend to purchase a 15% stake in WRB through the open market by March 2026. Because $69.41 represents a new record high driven largely by expected buying pressure rather than immediate fundamental earnings growth, there is a moderate risk of short-term mean reversion as existing shareholders take profits. I assign a probability of 0.44 for the first week (April 6) as the stock digests the initial pop, supported by yfinance's negative short-term trend following the break. Moving forward, the massive open-market purchases by MSI will supply persistent buying pressure. Combined with general market upward drift and an April 21 earnings report, the probabilities slowly shift to favor the upside, eventually reaching 0.58 over the 1-year horizon when MSI's purchase program concludes.
